title: rosa lutetiana var. biserrata
intro: Rosa lutetiana var. biserrata, also known as the Roman rose or sweetbriar, is a fragrant, deciduous shrub native to Europe with thorny stems and clusters of pink flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and astringent properties, which can help to heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and ease digestive issues. The health benefits of Rosa lutetiana var. biserrata include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also supporting immune function and reducing stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
